nodes.label: AI for production tasks (clipping, transcribing)
nodes.grounding_span: I mostly use it for social media content creation

nodes.label: AI for writing tasks (emails, captions, blogs)
nodes.grounding_span: Anything to do with writing, I always do myself

nodes.label: AI reliability and accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: Opus will glitch out and combine two different interview clips

nodes.label: AI expediting workflow
nodes.grounding_span: it has expedited processes that previously took a lot longer

nodes.label: Human touch in written content
nodes.grounding_span: I like to have a human touch on all our written content

nodes.label: Productivity and time efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: created more free time for me to engage with other work tasks

nodes.label: Authenticity and personal connection with students
nodes.grounding_span: the students deserve more nuanced, cultivated, and personal written content

nodes.label: Professional skill maintenance (writing, editing)
nodes.grounding_span: maintaining the personal element of my writing

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for production tasks
nodes.grounding_span: both consistently make me feel satisfied for the most part

nodes.label: Frustration with AI glitches
nodes.grounding_span: Opus will glitch out... That is frustrating

nodes.label: Resistance to AI for writing tasks
Openness to future AI use based on experience
nodes.grounding_span: it has made me re-evaluate my relationship with AI in a more positive light

nodes.label: Batch processing approach — uses AI intensively in phases
nodes.grounding_span: I go through phases where I'd use Opus a lot on a specific day/week

nodes.label: Trust-but-verify — sets parameters then reviews output
nodes.grounding_span: I usually just trust both softwares to do an accurate job, and I feel that I'd spot any mistakes
